points: Variation naturally accumulates over generations through reproduction; asexual reproduction produces only minor DNA copying inaccuracies, whereas sexual reproduction inherently maximises diversity.
Advantageous variations, such as heat resistance in bacteria, undergo environmental selection to critically secure species survival and form the basis of evolutionary processes.
Mendelian experiments proved that offspring inherit traits from equal genetic contributions of maternal and paternal DNA, strictly existing as two gene factors (e.g., TT or Tt).
Different traits are inherited entirely independently of each other; dominant traits visibly express even with a single copy, while recessive traits predictably require two identical copies.
Cellular DNA acts as the biological blueprint; specific genes functionally control characteristics by dictating the efficiency of protein production, which in turn regulates hormone levels.
Sex determination strategies vary biologically among species; in humans, it is strictly genetic and depends entirely on whether the father contributes an X (girl) or a Y (boy) chromosome.
